eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects tight uncertainty in forecast models for Istanbul's May 4 high temperature, with implied probabilities evenly split across 12–16°C as ensembles from ECMWF, GFS, and services like AccuWeather project peaks of 13–14°C. This positioning stems from a recent cool northerly airflow—driven by an upper-level trough over the Black Sea—delivering chilly air masses, combined with persistent cloud cover and breezy northeast winds (19–29 km/h) limiting solar heating and boundary-layer mixing. Light showers earlier in the week have reinforced the cool snap, well below May's climatological average of 20°C. Key differentiators include model spread on afternoon clearing potential and exact precipitation timing, which could nudge the high up or down by 1–2°C. Watch for fresh 12Z runs from NOAA and ECMWF, plus Turkish State Meteorological Service updates, expected within 24 hours.

markets.question: GPT-5.6 released by May 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if OpenAI's GPT-5.6 model is made available to the general public by the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

GPT-5.6 refers to a product explicitly named GPT-5.6, or a variant that is recognized as a direct successor to GPT-5.5, similar to the progression from GPT-5.1 to GPT-5.2. (e.g., GPT-5.7, GPT-5.8, etc., would qualify toward a "Yes" resolution to this market)

Qualifying releases of task-specialized models (e.g., GPT-Codex/Transcribe), cost-efficiency variants (e.g., Nano/Mini), or reasoning models of the o-series family will count for this market. Products labeled as a new flagship generation GPT-6 or similar will NOT qualify.

For this market to resolve to "Yes," a qualifying model must be launched and publicly accessible, including via open beta or open rolling waitlist signups. A closed beta or any form of private access will not suffice. 

The release must be either clearly defined and publicly announced by OpenAI as being accessible to the general public or otherwise made publicly accessible and explicitly labeled within the company’s official website. Labeling errors, placeholder text, or version names displayed on the website that do not correspond to a model that is actually accessible to the general public will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from OpenAI, with additional verification from a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: OpenAI's recent release of GPT-5.5 on April 23, 2026—its most agentic large language model yet, with native computer use and faster coding—has accelerated trader expectations for GPT-5.6, spotted in internal Codex logs shortly after, signaling active testing amid a compressing release cycle from GPT-5's August 2025 debut. Competitive dynamics intensify pressure, as OpenAI signals "significant short-term improvements" and hints at rapid iteration to counter Google's Gemini advancements ahead of Google I/O on May 19. Traders eye mid-June as a likely window based on 28- to 49-day historical gaps, with public availability to general users as the key resolution criterion; delays could stem from safety red-teaming or regulatory scrutiny.

markets.question: Will New England Patriots win the 2026 AFC East?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the team that is declared as the AFC East division champion for the 2026 NFL regular season.

If multiple teams tie atop the division, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the NFL.

If the 2026 NFL regular season is cancelled, postponed after January 17, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared for this division within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NFL; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Series: Anyone's Legend Kill Handicap (-10.5) vs Weibo Gaming (+10.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Anyone's Legend and Weibo Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, scheduled for January 20 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend secures 11 or more kills than Weibo Gaming in the match. 

Otherwise, it will resolve to "Weibo Gaming".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong Observatory's latest 9-day forecast, updated at 16:30 HKT on May 2, projects a maximum temperature of 28°C on May 3 under mainly cloudy skies with morning sunny intervals giving way to showers and squally thunderstorms from a developing cold front over central China. High humidity (75-95%) and shifting winds—from southerly force 3 to northerly force 4-5—will suppress daytime heating, fostering trader consensus around 28-29°C with near-even implied probabilities. Morning sun may enable brief spikes to 29°C during peak insolation around midday, but afternoon precipitation risks capping it lower; model consensus reflects this uncertainty ahead of overnight updates as the front nears Guangdong. May climatology supports highs near 28-30°C amid normal to above-normal seasonal temperatures.

markets.question: Will Kim Jong Un be the next leader out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the first individual who ceases to occupy their listed office.

An announcement of a resignation/removal, or a scheduled departure from office due to the outcome of an election, will not alone qualify.

Only permanent removal from office will qualify for resolution. Temporary removals, such as impeachment suspensions (e.g., Yoon Suk Yeol's recent impeachment), temporary invocation of the 25th Amendment, or any similar provisional transfers of power, will not count.

Additionally, if an individual continues in a caretaker or interim role (e.g., Gabriel Attal remaining as caretaker Prime Minister of France), they will not be considered to have ceased occupying the office for the purposes of this market.

If this criterion has not been met for any of the listed individuals by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “None before 2027”. No additional individuals will be added to this market after its creation.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hungary's parliamentary election on April 12, 2026, delivered a landslide victory for Péter Magyar's centre-right Tisza party over Viktor Orbán's Fidesz, with record 79.5% turnout fueled by economic woes, corruption scandals, and demands for stronger EU ties. Orbán promptly conceded defeat, ending his 16-year tenure as prime minister and paving the way for a swift government transition. This outcome anchors trader consensus at 94.8% for Orbán exiting power before 2027, embodying the wisdom of crowds in pricing near-certainty post-election. Realistic challenges include Fidesz-led recounts, constitutional disputes, or prolonged handover delays, though Tisza's supermajority in the National Assembly renders these unlikely absent major legal reversals.

eventMetadata.context_description: Manchester City's home Premier League clash with Crystal Palace, originally set for March 21 at Etihad Stadium, was postponed due to City's participation in the Carabao Cup final against Arsenal the following day, with no rescheduled date confirmed yet—likely in late April or May midweeks. Sitting second in the table with 61 points from 30 matches, nine behind leaders Arsenal, City view this game in hand as pivotal for their title push amid a tight top-four race including Manchester United and Aston Villa. Palace, mid-table battlers, face defensive injury woes with Daniel Munoz, Cheick Doucoure, and Jean Mateta sidelined long-term, while City manage knocks to Nico O'Reilly and Josko Gvardiol alongside Mateo Kovacic's Achilles absence. Recent City form shows rotation depth, but home advantage and superior goal difference favor them upon rescheduling.

Trader consensus favors Ilia Topuria at 52.5% implied probability to claim the top spot in 2026, driven by his perfect 17-0 record, knockout wins over Holloway and Oliveira en route to the lightweight title, and his imminent return against Justin Gaethje on June 14 following a brief early-year hiatus for personal reasons—positioning him for a potential stylistic striking clash with Makhachev. Islam/None in 2026 trades at 40%, reflecting his grappling mastery and injury-delayed August return, while Dricus du Plessis (17.2%) and Alex Pereira (16%) draw support from middleweight and light heavyweight title defenses amid rising momentum.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no criminal charges against Les Wexner by June 30, reflecting the absence of any DOJ indictment or FBI action despite heightened scrutiny from February 2026 Epstein file releases revealing past investigations into his ties with Jeffrey Epstein. Wexner, deposed by the House Oversight Committee that month, denied knowledge of Epstein's crimes, claimed he was duped, and emphasized severing ties upon discovering misconduct; no charges followed. Recent civil lawsuits filed in early April by Epstein survivors alleging funding of crimes have sustained media attention but do not signal imminent prosecution, amid potential statute of limitations barriers for historical allegations. Late-breaking evidence or new probes could shift odds, though none appear scheduled.

markets.question: Will Argentina’s annual inflation in 2026 be between 30% and 34.9%?
markets.description:
This is a market about Argentinian inflation over the 12-month period ending December 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the National Institute of Statistics and Census (INDEC) of Argentina.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI / IPC) over the 12-month period ending in December 2026 (Variación % interanual Total nacional) according to the monthly INDEC report.

The resolution source for this market will be the INDEC Consumer Price Index report released for December 2026 (https://www.indec.gob.ar/), expected to be released in January 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report by clicking on the “Precios al consumidor” option on the home page of https://www.indec.gob.ar/, and searching the pdf for the figure under “Variación % interanual Total nacional”.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly INDEC CPI (IPC) news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 33.6%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices Argentina's 2026 annual CPI inflation in the 25-29.9% (36%) and 30-34.9% (33.5%) bins nearly evenly, reflecting uncertainty in the disinflation trajectory after March's 3.4% monthly print—the highest of 2026—lifted Q1 cumulative inflation to 9.4% and exceeded expectations, prompting upward revisions to full-year forecasts near 30% per Reuters poll median and IMF at 30.4%. Milei's fiscal austerity and monetary restraint have slowed annual rates to 32.6% YoY, but persistent core pressures from wages and regulated prices fuel debate on sustained sub-3% monthly readings. April CPI, due mid-May, represents the key swing factor, with private estimates eyeing 2.4-2.7% to affirm cooling momentum.

markets.question: Will White House post 180-199 posts from May 1 to May 8, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times White House (@WhiteHouse), posts on X between May 1, 12:00 PM ET and May 8, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will Federico Valverde win the 2026 Ballon d'Or?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 Ballon d’Or.

If no 2026 Ballon d’Or winner is declared by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from France Football (https://www.francefootball.fr/).

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Harry Kane tops trader consensus for the 2026 Ballon d'Or after a record-breaking 2025-26 campaign at Bayern Munich, where his 54 goals and league-leading hat-tricks across 46 appearances propelled the Bundesliga champions deep into Champions League semifinals, including a starring role in a recent quarterfinal classic. Teammate Michael Olise's explosive right-wing form—four UCL goals and seven assists—has rocketed him to second, reflecting Bayern's dominance in Europe's top competition. Kylian Mbappé has slipped amid Real Madrid's inconsistent La Liga and UCL showings, while Ousmane Dembélé and Lamine Yamal hold firm on PSG's Ligue 1 surge and Barcelona's youth-driven attack, respectively; Declan Rice and Vitinha benefit from Arsenal and PSG midfield mastery. With the World Cup looming, UCL final outcomes and international exploits could reshape probabilities.

markets.question: Stevenage FC vs. Wigan Athletic FC: Both Teams to Score
markets.description:
In the upcoming League One game between Stevenage FC and Wigan Athletic FC, scheduled for May 2 at 10:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Stevenage FC and Wigan Athletic FC each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on efl.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Stevenage FC vs. Wigan Athletic FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the League One game, scheduled for May 2 at 10:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Stevenage host Wigan Athletic on the final day of the League One season at Lamex Stadium, with Boro chasing a playoff spot around sixth in the table while Wigan sit mid-table in 13th. Stevenage's momentum faltered in a 5-1 midweek thrashing by Bolton Wanderers on April 14, exposing defensive vulnerabilities amid injuries to defender Lewis Freestone and lingering absences like striker Harry Cornick. Wigan, steadier with mixed recent results including a 3-1 win over Northampton, face absences of midfielder Baba Adeeko and defender Llyton Chapman for the campaign. Stevenage hold the edge in recent head-to-heads, winning the last two encounters, bolstered by home form in a high-stakes finale where results elsewhere could sway postseason implications.

markets.question: Will Matteo Berrettini be the 2026 Men’s Wimbledon winner?
markets.description:
Wimbledon 2026 is scheduled for June 29 - July 12, 2026.

This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 Wimbledon Men’s Singles Tournament.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 Wimbledon Men’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 Wimbledon Men’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after August 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from Wimbledon (https://www.wimbledon.com/index.html); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jannik Sinner leads trader consensus at 48% implied probability for the 2026 Men's Wimbledon winner, fueled by his world No. 1 status and dominant 28-2 record through early May, including Masters 1000 titles at Indian Wells, Miami, and a record-breaking run to the Madrid final just days ago—matching feats once held by Federer, Djokovic, and Nadal. Carlos Alcaraz sits second at 18.5% after completing his career Grand Slam with the Australian Open triumph in February, though recent losses to Sinner on clay temper expectations ahead of grass. Novak Djokovic's 6.8% reflects ongoing shoulder injury woes, multiple withdrawals like Monte Carlo, and age-related concerns at 38, despite his seven Wimbledon titles, as Alexander Zverev rounds out the top tier at 3.7% on steady No. 3 ranking form. With grass-court season imminent post-French Open, surface transitions and fitness will sharpen these probabilities.

markets.question: Will S&P 500 (SPX) hit $8,200 (HIGH) in December?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point between market creation and market close on the final day of trading for December 2026, any 1-minute candle for S&P 500 (SPX) shows a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

All prices recorded during regular trading hours of the primary exchange for the instrument, as reflected in Yahoo Finance's 1-minute interval ("1m") data, will be considered.

Periods when the market is officially closed (e.g., holidays or maintenance breaks) will not be considered.

All times referenced are local to the primary exchange on which the index trades.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the 1-minute interval ("1m") chart data for S&P 500 (SPX) available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/%5EGSPC/.

events.title: What will S&P 500 (SPX) hit by end of December?
events.description: What will S&P 500 (SPX) hit by end of December 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: The S&P 500 has surged to fresh record highs near 7,230 as of May 1, 2026, propelled by stronger-than-expected Q1 earnings growth and resilient corporate margins amid AI-driven productivity gains. Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects this momentum, pricing an 79% implied probability of hitting 7,400 by year-end, with 52% for 7,600, backed by real capital wagering on continued economic expansion. Supporting factors include stable unemployment around 4.3% and March CPI showing moderated food inflation at 2.7% year-over-year, though energy pressures persist. Key catalysts ahead: April CPI release on May 12, June 16-17 FOMC meeting for potential rate cut signals, and Q2 earnings season, all pivotal for valuation multiples currently at a forward P/E of 20.9.

markets.question: Will Iván Cepeda Castro place 2nd in the 1st round of the 2026 Colombian presidential election?
markets.description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who receives the second-most valid votes in the first round of this election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the results of the first round of the Colombian presidential election are not known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls from Atlas Intel (April 30) and Invamer position independent conservative Abelardo de la Espriella ahead of Centro Democrático Senator Paloma Valencia for second place in Colombia's May 31 first-round presidential election, with de la Espriella at 21-22% and Valencia near 20%, trailing leftist leader Iván Cepeda (37-44%). This trader consensus, reflecting skin-in-the-game bets, stems from de la Espriella's momentum in Google Trends across 32 departments and consistent polling edges post-March primaries, where Valencia secured her party's nomination amid legislative voting. The tight contest for a runoff spot hinges on undecided voters, turnout in battlegrounds, and pre-election violence concerns, keeping odds fluid under 55% for the frontrunner.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus gives Lady Gaga a slim edge at the 2026 Met Gala on May 4, fueled by persistent rumors of her red carpet return after a seven-year absence, tied to promoting her cameo in The Devil Wears Prada 2. A Vanity Fair journalist's early April report ignited buzz, with recent amplification from her confirmed sighting heading to New York City on May 2—aligning perfectly with promotional timing amid the Costume Art theme. Absent an official statement from Gaga or her team, however, traders remain cautious given her history of selective appearances and last-minute shifts in celebrity schedules. The red carpet livestream could deliver final clarity as skin-in-the-game bets hinge on verified attendance.

markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

eventMetadata.context_description: EUR/USD trades near 1.172 after recent Federal Reserve and ECB policy holds, with the Fed maintaining its 3.50%-3.75% federal funds target range at the April 28-29 FOMC meeting and the ECB keeping the deposit facility rate at 2.00% on April 30 amid rising inflation pressures—US March CPI accelerated to 3.3% year-over-year, while Eurozone April flash CPI hit 3.0%. Divergent paths underpin trader consensus, as markets price in potential Fed rate cuts later in 2026 against ECB's hawkish tilt on sticky prices and meager 0.1% Q1 eurozone growth. USD gained from Trump tariff threats and Iran tensions, but euro rebounded from April lows near 1.15. Watch US April CPI on May 12 and June 10 ECB/16-17 FOMC meetings for rate path shifts.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 1.25% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve held the federal funds target range steady at 3.50%-3.75% in its April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting—the third consecutive pause—amid hotter-than-expected March CPI inflation at 3.3% year-over-year, up sharply from 2.4%, signaling sticky price pressures despite resilient labor markets with unemployment dipping to 4.3%. Trader consensus via CME FedWatch Tool prices near-certainty (over 98%) of no June cut, with 30-day Fed funds futures implying a gradual path toward the mid-3% range by year-end 2026, balancing inflation trajectory against economic strength. Key catalysts include April CPI on May 12 and the June 16-17 FOMC, where sustained disinflation could unlock 25-basis-point easing.

events.title: Chelsea FC vs. Nottingham Forest FC - Halftime Result
events.description: In the upcoming Premier League game between Chelsea FC and Nottingham Forest FC, scheduled for May 4, 2026 at 10:00 AM ET: This event contains halftime result markets for home, draw, and away outcomes within the first 45 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a razor-thin contest for the halftime result in Chelsea's home Premier League clash with Nottingham Forest, with all outcomes at 50% amid widespread injury doubts on both sides. Chelsea, sitting sixth in the table, face defensive headaches without Reece James, Levi Colwill, and Trevoh Chalobah, while João Pedro and Estevão remain fitness concerns after recent thigh and hamstring issues; Enzo Fernández is expected back from a calf knock. Forest, buoyed by Europa League semifinal progress but ninth in standings, counter with their own absences including Chris Wood, Callum Hudson-Odoi, and Murillo, yet boast resilient away form that has frustrated top sides. Cautious opening exchanges in prior head-to-heads further even the implied probabilities, highlighting lineup uncertainties and mutual vulnerabilities.

markets.question: Will the next diplomatic US-Iran meeting be in the UAE?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the country in which the next diplomatic meeting between government representatives of the United States and Iran takes place by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify. 

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not count.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

If the next diplomatic meeting between government representatives of the United States and Iran takes place in any country in the Middle East or North Africa other than the listed options, this market will resolve to “Other - Middle East/North Africa”. 

If the next diplomatic meeting between government representatives of the United States and Iran takes place in any country in Europe other than the listed options, this market will resolve to “Other - Europe”.

For the purposes of this market, additional countries’ regions will be determined based on the US State Department’s regional classifications in the “Countries and Areas List” (https://www.state.gov/countries-and-areas-list). Any country classified as part of “Europe and Eurasia” will be considered to be in Europe. Any country classified as part of “Near East (Middle East and North Africa)” will be considered to be in the Middle East.

If the next diplomatic meeting between government representatives of the United States and Iran takes place in any unlisted country which is not classified in either of the specified regions, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If no qualifying meeting takes place by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No Meeting by June 30”.

If a qualifying meeting occurs in more than one country, resolution will be based on where the first qualifying diplomatic session takes place.

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the governments of the United States and Iran, and a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Pakistan leads trader consensus at 65% implied probability as the venue for the next US-Iran diplomatic meeting, reflecting its pivotal mediation role in brokering the April 8 ceasefire amid the 2026 Iran conflict and hosting initial indirect talks in Islamabad earlier this month, where US Vice President JD Vance engaged counterparts for 21 hours. White House statements on April 15 affirmed Pakistan as the "only mediator," sustaining optimism despite President Trump's late-April cancellation of envoy trips by Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, which elevated "No Meeting by June 30" to 28.5% amid rejected Iranian proposals and softened conditions. Prior venues like Oman and Geneva (Switzerland) linger at low odds, overshadowed by Islamabad's channel; fragile ceasefire deadlines may dictate progress.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NY-13 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-13 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: New York's 13th Congressional District, with a Cook Partisan Voting Index of D+32, has consistently delivered lopsided Democratic victories—over 80% margins in recent generals, including Rep. Adriano Espaillat's 83% win in 2024—driving trader consensus to price Democrats at 93.5% for the November 3, 2026, general election. The district's urban, low-income Upper Manhattan and Bronx demographics favor Democrats overwhelmingly, as seen in 2024 presidential results (Harris 79%). While the June 23 Democratic primary features a crowded field challenging Espaillat, including DSA-backed Darializa Avila Chevalier—who polled competitively in an April internal survey—GOP candidates remain marginal, with no credible contender filed. Scenarios to upend this include a post-primary Democratic scandal, nominee health crisis, or unprecedented national Republican wave.
